rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving type errors in assume/guarantee expressions, ensuring all identifiers are visible and properly typed.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when necessary.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, trace the root cause to unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations and correct them.
Ensure that component type contracts only reference features visible at the type scope, and use implementation annexes for subcomponent-specific bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and use valid AADL identifiers.
Assumptions and guarantees must have expressions of the correct type (e.g., bool for boolean expressions).
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE type errors; instead, fix or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Component type vs. implementation scope for ports and features
